column1:
"
generate_dataset.py
--------------------
PURPOSE:
This script generates a realistic, statistically-structured dataset that
mirrors the schema and behaviour of tweets collected via the scraping
pipeline in `scrape_twitter.py`.

WHY A GENERATED DATASET IS USED HERE:
Live scraping of X/Twitter requires either (a) official API credentials
(Tweepy + Bearer Token) or (b) outbound access to twitter.com / x.com /
nitter mirrors. The sandboxed analysis environment used to build this
submission only allows a restricted set of network domains (PyPI, GitHub,
npm, etc.) and has no social-media API keys configured. To keep the EDA
and Visualization tasks fully reproducible end-to-end, this script builds
a synthetic-but-realistic dataset with the SAME columns, distributions,
and noise characteristics you would expect from a real scrape on the
topic Electric Vehicle (EV) Adoption". When you run `scrape_twitter.py`

column2: it writes a CSV with an identical schema

column1: Topic chosen: Public conversation around Electric Vehicle (EV) adoption
covering India and global markets - a realistic

"
